VerbalDescription: copy grid size. keep the red line in the same space. move the blue shape until it touches the red line.
Move the blue shape over as close to the red to be resting next to it. I copied the grid then used edit, fill and coloring to match and move the shape over.
Use the same grid size.  There will be two objects in the input.  Move the smaller object so that it touches the larger object that spans the entire grid. Keep the colors of the objects the same.
copy the input grid size. shift the blue shape one box to the right. that's it.

Resize the grid reflect the grid given. Recreate the line of red cells inside of the grid as they are in the first grid. Without changing the orientation of the blue cells (only moving them up, down, left, or right) shift the object composed of blue cells so that there is no gap of black cells between them. (If the blue cells are not solid [i.e. a square] and make up a shape that has a gap, only the blue cells reaching the furthest out need to touch the red cells. Do so without overlapping the red and blue cells.
Use same size grid. Move the blue shape within same row and adjacent of the red horizontal line.
For this one, I had to remove the black space inbetween the blue shape and the red line so that both shapes were touching. no colour change is needed for either shape.
Move the blue figure to the right until it touches the red figure.

Use the same grid size as the input. The input features a blue shape and a red shape, separated by an empty row or column. Shift the blue shape by ONE row OR column, as needed, so that the blue shape is touching the red shape.
keep the grid the same size and the orange line in place, then butt the blue object up against the orange line.
Recreate the input grid identically, the rule is to move the blue shape in the input grid so as to be touching the red line provided. Keep the red line unmoved.

Resize the grid to the format of the grid given. Recreate the red line of the grid as it is in the first grid. 

Place the blue cells so that the furthest reaching parts of the blue cell shape/object makes contact with the red line (but not overlapping). Keep the blue cell shape/object in the same color and orientation (no rotation or color change).

Use the same grid size as in the input. Shift each grid of the blue shape in the test input grid until the blue shape touches the vertical red shape. Do not touch the red shape keep it in the same position.
Shift the blocks on the left so that it will make contact with the one blocks on the right.
Keep the grid size the same. Shift the blue pattern by one block so that it touches the red line.
Keep the grid size the same as the input grid. Shift the blue pattern over so that it's touching the red line.

Create the same grid size. Include the red line of squares exactly where it is on the original but move the blue shape exactly one square towards the red line. Keep the blue shape identical in every way but just move it one square in the direction of the red line

Make the grid the same size as the task image. You will want to move the blue blocks over to the right so that the out 'edge' of the pattern of the blue blocks touches/is next to - without a gap - the vertical line of red blocks. So, look at your pattern of blue blocks. The furthest block or blocks to the right will be the blocks you need to move right next to the vertical line of red blocks. Do not change the pattern of the blue blocks. Imagine your blue blocks are a complete pattern and they are docking with the red line of blocks.

Ensure your grid matches the test condition and not the demonstration. Your goal is to 'move' the blue squares, as an object, to touch the red line of squares. For example, if there is a red vertical line to the right of your grid - it doesn't matter where - and there is a number of blue squares to its left, then consider the blue squares as a whole object and move them to the right, keeping the shape of the object, until the first column of blue squares is next to the red line of squares.
Move the blue object so that it is touching the red line.
Keeping the same grid size, leave the red line in the same spot and shift the blue shape over in the same orientation until it makes contact with the red line.
Find the blue shape and shift it towards the red line until it is touching it.

resize the new grid to same dimensions as old one. 
the old grid will have a blue shape and a red line.  
draw the red line in the same location as the old grid
move the blue shape either horizontally or vertically until it is touching the red line.
Copy the grid. Move the blue shape till it touches the red line.

First, resize the grid to be the same as the original. There will be two shapes on the original. Shift the left most shape over to the left until the left edge is touching the edge of the grid and then recreate the shape from that starting point. Then move the right most shape over to the right edge and recreate the shape so it is touching the right edge.

There will be two different colored objects in the input. Use the same grid size and object colors as the input, but separate the objects apart horizontally as far as possible by shifting the first object to the leftmost edge and shifting the other object to the rightmost edge of the grid. Do not adjust vertical position.

Using the same sized grid as the sample, move each colored shape all the way to the right or all the way to the left so that each image is reproduced in the same color and size as in the sample. If a colored image is closer to the left, move the image all the way to the left so its left-most edge is in the left-most column but the colored image remains otherwise identical, and if a colored image is closer to the right, move the colored image all the way to the right so its right-most edge is in the right-most column but the colored image remains otherwise identical.
Copy the grid size, but move the 2 shapes from the center to each of their respective edges.
The two original objust are the same color and size but spread on the opposite ends as far as possible while maintaining their original shape.

Use the same grid size as the input. There will be 2 objects in the input. In your answer, shift the object on the left as far left as possible, and the object on the right as far right as possible. Keep them the same colour
Copy the proportions of the original demo. move both objects outwards so that they touch each outtermost edge. Keep colour and proportion the same.

Use the same grid size as in the input. Consider the shapes in the grid and transpose them by shifting them in the opposite cardinal direction from each other until the reach the opposite sides/walls. The colours remain the same.

Keep the grid size and colors of the objects the same. Just push the objects to the edges of the left and right of the grid. The left object gets pushed to the left edge of the grid and the right object gets pushed to the right edge of the grid. Everything is the same other than that.
Copy from the input grid. Move the leftmost object over to the farthest left edge, and move the rightmost object over to the farthest right edge.
Adjust grid to example. Do not change size or color. Take each shape and move them to opposite sides.

Copy the grid size. Then, look at the two shapes. Move these shapes to the side, with the left shape going to the left as far as it can go, and the right shape to the right side as far as it can go (the shapes should be in the same colour  they are as in the original picture)
grid remains the same, blocks all moved over to the closest of left/right until a block is touching edges
Move each shape to the outer edges. They must stay at the same height as they originally were
set grid to same dimensions (8x25). same colours. place red object in the same place. move green object the farthest right placement.
There are two distinct shapes. Move the shapes as far away from each other (along the horizontal axis only) so that both shapes are touching the edges of the black space.
move the objects as far away from eachother to the other side of the grid
Copy input, move each pattern to opposite ends as far as they can go

Resize the grid to the same size as input, copy the shapes size and colour put the left one against the left side of the grid and the right one against the right side of the grid. Keep the vertical positioning of the shapes the same.

Copy the input grid. Then shift the verticle or horizontal lines to the closest edge of the grid. Ensure the lines are standing up rather than laying down. The lines can be on either wall of the grid.
Move every single square as close to the nearest edge as it can get, filling in any gaps between.

for this one, if the coloured lines were vertical, you would need to shift each of the vertical lines down the to bottom of the grid (as if gravity had just pulled the tiles down). and if the lines were horizontal you would need to shift them all to the right hand side of the grip (as if a magnet had been applied on the right hand side of the grid and pull each line towards it).
Count the number of squares in each column. Then move it to the nearest edge leaving no gaps in between squares

There are line running either vertically or horizontally in the input. The output grid is the same size as the input with those lines moved either down in the vertical case or right in the horizontal case. They should be moved as far as the can go until one block reaches the bottom or another (moved) line.

Make the grid the same size as the original. There will be single lines as objects. If the objects are horizontal you will slide all the objects to the right and keep the color the same. If the objects are vertical then you need to slide all the objects to the bottom of the grid. If two shapes are in the same row or column then they get stack on top of each other making a longer line. Keep all the shapes the same.

Resize grid to match one on left. Imagine all blocks are falling like in the game Tetris. Use select to create an image of how the blocks would look like if they feel straight down. Pretend the bottom of the grid is the floor. Just straight down.

Keep the grid size & colour the same. 
If the lines of cells are running vertically, bring them all the way down to the bottom, do not include the gaps between the cells in that row. If the lines/cells are running horizontally, bring them all the way to the right hand side.
move all the squares to the bottom of the grid keeping them is the same line that they are in and in the same colour
take all the columns and slide them down the grid so they stack on one another . Keep grid size and color the same

Use the same grid size. There should be several lines in orange. If they are horizontal, shift them over all the way to the right side of the grid. If they are vertical, shift them all the way down to the bottom of the grid. No overlapping.
choose the color and fill the box based on grids
xfhrf

The grid is 10 x 10. If the lines in the original are vertical, imagine they are falling down to the base. The lines retain their length and colour and their position on the x axis, just now the lowermost square touches the bottom edge, and there are no vertical gaps between the squares. Likewise, if the lines are horizontal, the same applies except the blocks all shift against the right side, again with no gaps, so the right most block in the original touches the right side.
Grid is the same size as example. Size and color of shapes do not change. Move shapes all to side they are gathere

Use the same size grid as the input. There will be various rows/columns in the input. Make sure that all the coloured squares meet together towards the right. All the orange squares should be next to each other if there are gaps in between them. Only put how many orange squares there are in the input next to each other. Do not fill in any extra squares.

Press the copy from input button. If you have rows of colour, then shift each row all the way to the right. You should not show any gaps. If you have columns of colour, then shift each column all the way to the bottom. You should not show any gaps
Use the same grid size as the input. Keeping them in the same row, move all of the orange squares to the right making sure they touch each other.
Same grid. Move the piece right to touch the right edge of the grid.

Start by copying from input. You need to draw the shape all the way to the right side of the grid. Match the colour of the shape and copy the shape so that the outside right edge of the shape is all the way the farthest side on the right of the grid that it can be. Keep the shape on the same row as the input.
Move the inputted shape to the far right, keeping size and color.
copied the pattern and mopved to touch the right hand side
Shape moved to the right until touching right wall
You will see a shape. You are to move the shape all the way to the right of the grid at the same height it is in the example. The shape should remain the same otherwise.
Shift the entire shape to the far right so that it touches the right edge of the grid.  Keep the color the same.
Grid size remains the same. Move the test input to the extreme end of the grid by the right. test input colour remain the same
Grid size remain the same as the test input. Move the coloured grid input to the extreme end of the grid by the Right. Grid colours remain thesame as the test input
Adjust grid to example size. Do not change any shapes color or size. Take all shapes and move them to the right as far as possible.
Use the same grid size as the input grid. Copy the shapes, and redraw them like a magnet is pulling them to the right wall of the grid.

Look at the input shapes, your answer will be the same grid size as the input and the shapes are all shifted to the right until the right most block of the shape touches the right hand side of the grid. The shape will be the same size and colour as the input.
Use the same grid size as the input. There will be two object in the input, in your answer, shift the object to the edge of the grid to the right ( keep its colour the same).
copy from the input and move all the object towards the left.
Use the same grid size. Shift every colored block all the way to the left, but do not overlap and instead stop it behind the colored block

Make the grid 10 x 10. Shift all of the shapes to the very left wall, keeping them at the same height. If one shape will run into the other, keeping it at the same height, place it in the next cell directly to the right. They will touch each other.

Use the same grid size as the input. Shift all objects on the grid towards the left until they make contact with the leftmost side or another shape, whichever comes first. The objects should never overlap, intersect, or move through each other. All colours remain the same.
Use the same size grid (10x10). Copy the same shapes except move the whole large pink shape one spot to the left so that it attackes to the single pink block on the left edge.
Move objects as far left as possible without changing their original size, shape etc. If one part of an object has moved as far left as possible, the rest stays attached and in place.
Keep the grid and the colors the same. Objects stay the same just move every thing to the left as far as it can go
Match the grid size of the original. Keep the same colour but shift the whole object to the left side, leaving no spaces between the blocks.

Ensure that the black grid is the same size as the input.  Shift everything over to the extreme left.  Black spaces can be taken up but pink blocks cannot be over written and solid shapes cannot be broken up.
Keep the grid size & colour the same. Move each cell one by one to the very left hand side of the table, keeping within its previous horizontal line. Leave no gaps
All the blocks go to the left, make a 10x10 grid and then from the top left go down 2 and put a pink box and go 6 of them across and then 6 down then 3 across or something like that good luck
Copy the grid and move every cell to the left as much as you can.
move everything to the left until it cannot move any longer
Make sure the grid is 10x10.  Instead of falling to the bottom and piling up, imagine them falling to the left and piling up and draw that into the grid.  Use the same color as the source.
shift all the colored grids to the right
take the shapes and move them directly to the left hand side of the grid, keep the colour the same.
grid remains same, shapes move left completely to edge along the same axis - colours remain the same and the individual shapes merge
Use the same grid size. Observe the task demonstration. Shift the object to the extreme left. Keep the colour the same.
resize grid to 10x10, draw a pink line starting at 4 down, move across 9 squares. draw a vertical pink line at 6 down, moving down 5 squares, to the bottom of the grid.

The solution to this puzzle is to shift all shapes as far to the left of the grid as possible. So for example, if there is a row of 5 pink cells, which are 3 cells in from the left hand side of the grid, this row of 5 pink cells should be shifted (while remaining on the same row of the grid), to be against the left hand side of the grid.

Kepp the grid size the same. Imagine the image is the letter T.  If this letter was the correct way up it will be moved down until it reaches the edge of the grid.  If the T is not the right way up it will be moved in the direction of the bottom of the T until it reaches the edge of the grid.  Keep the colour the same.
Use the same size grid as the original. There is one object. The object is yellow and this remains unchanged. Shift the object up by two grid squares.

Keep the grid size the same from the input. There will be a T shape. Keep the orientation the same (it may be sideways or in a regular T shape). Move the shape so the longer line of the T is touching the edge of the grid.

use the same size grid as the input. wherever the bottom side of the "T" shape is pointing, move it three squares to that side. the bottom side of the "T" shape will be connected to the end of the grid. use the same colour and size of the "T" shape

Keep the grid size the same as shown. You will see a shape, which looks like the letter T. You need to translate this shape until it reaches the edge of the grid. You need to translate it in the direction of the stem of the T shape.
Copy the exact same grid from the original, using the same color move the shape so the point of the tail is touching the edge it is pointing towards. The shape should be the same.
First change the size as 10. Then click resize. Then move the color grid as they did in the demonstration.
Make the grid the same size (10x10) and shift the shape so that the horizontal line meets the edge of the grid. Keep the shape the same colour.
Use the same grid as the input and also the same colour as the letter T in the input. Make sure the bottom of the T is touching the end of the grid by filling those squares in red.

In this case the problem seems to be that the shape moves right to the end of the 10x10 grid in the direction it is pointing. In this case the shape was pointing to the left, so I moved it right to the end of the grid on the left hand side

To do this, I resized the grid to 10X10 and recreated the shape with the same dimensions on the left extremity of the grid. This involved using the edit tool to recreate the T shape using the red colour.
See how the shape is like a T? Move the T downwards towards the way the bottom of the T shape is pointing until it reaches the end of the grid.

Copy the input grid. The design needs to shift in the direction of the "arrow" or "t" is pointing, or the direction of the shape in general. The distance it needs to shift - count how many squares are between the edge of the grid and the start of the design. Double this number. (i.e. if there's one square between the edge and the object, the number you need when doubled is 2). Then, move the object over by that many squares from it's starting position (in this example, 2)

Resize the grid to the same size as input, the shape will be a T shape, if it is sitting vertically put it on the bottom of the grid, but copy it's horizonal position. If it is sitting horizontally (on it's side), position is horizontally on the edge of the grid (left or right depending on the direction the T is in), keep the vertical position the same. Use the same colour.

Copy from input, Wherever the tail part of the T is pointing (like the end of a corkscrew opener) move the shape to the end of the grid, So if the tail of the T is pointing right move the shape all the way to the right, if its pointing up move it all the way to the top e.c.t, 
so perfectly Select the T shape using select option press c and then reset the grid to remove the image then select the correct square according to the above rule (top left is the reference point) and press v
Move all the T to the end line  where the pointy part of the T pointing to

The grid size should match the input. Each shape should be preserved but moved to the edge of the grid in which its facing and oriented, so the letter "T" would move straight down, or a left-facing T would move all the way to the left.

Create a grid with the same dimensions as the test input grid. Move the top t-shape to the right until it touches the edge of the grid. Move the bottom t-shape left until it touches the edge of the grid.
Delete the yellow line and leave the grid the same size. Then move the blue block to the opposite wall that it is on but in the same spot.
The yellow line is the points in the direction in which the blue square flips.

The rule is that the yellow line should be removed, and the blue square moved to the opposite side of the grid to where it started. You should resize the grid to 6 x 6. Then ise the edit button to  change the yellow squares to black. Then click on edit and select the blue, and place a blue grid square at the opposite side of the grid, parallel to where the yellow line was. The go to the iringinal boue grid square and change it to black using the edit function - click on edit, select black, then click on the original blue grid square to change it to black.

First, identify the yellow bar and recolour it entirely in black to match the background. Then identify the position of the blue square. Move the blue square to the direct opposite 'end' of the grid in a cardinal direction i.e. top to bottom, left to right. Blue squares in corners are moved to the cardinal direction that was not formally occupied by the yellow bar.
Copy the grid. Move the single square to the opposite side of the grid, parallel to the solid line. Then remove the line by changing it to black.

Start by making the grid the same size as the input. The rule is to mirror the shape so as to move the blue square on to the opposite side wall. Once you have done this simply delete the yellow column and that will be your answer.
Keep same grid size. Remove yellow line. Move blue square to the opposite side that it's touching (eg if it's touching top move it to the bottom). Keep same colour

Ensure that the black grid is the same size as the input.  Delete the yellow line.  Move the blue square to the other edge of the black grid following the axis (horizontal or vertical) that the yellow line ran along.
Copy the size of the grid exactly, remove the long yellow line and change to black, move the single blue square from the very bottom to the very top
Adjust the image to be the dimensions of the test. Do not copy over the yellow line leave this blank / black. Wherever the blue square is. Flip this horizontally

Choose 'Copy from input' button. Choose 'Select' and draw a box that contains all of the cells of the long shape. Click the black color to erase the shape. Choose 'Edit' and choose the color of the single cell shape. Click the grid to make a single cell shape that is on the opposite end of the grid, as if you moved the shape in a straight line. Choose the black color and click on the original single cell shape to erase it.
Find out the least number of filled grids. Fill the same color and select the opposite side of the corresponding grid.

Set the grid size to the same as the input grid. The input features one "bar" shape and one single square. The single square borders an edge of the grid. "Mirror" the single square so that it now occupies the same position on the opposite edge of the grid. If the single square borders two edges (ie. in a corner), choose the corner that is "empty" in the input image. Use the same color. The answer grid should contain one square colored square, bordering the edge opposite of its input position (top/bottom or left/right).
Comp grid from input. Remove the line of squares. Use the select tool to input a square on the opposite side the single square is on. Then use the edit tool to remove the original square.
With the lone filled cell, fill the opposite side of the row/column with same color. Fill the original shape cell black. Make grid same size.

Press the copy from input button. Change the colour of the top row to whatever the test input shows to black. Then move the colour square to where it would be when mirrowed, and remove the other colour square that came across from the test input grid.
Remove the long line and replace it with black. Move the single colour square to its mirrored position, e.g. if it is originally bottom right then put it bottom left.
Keep the large shape where it is. Move the smaller shape in a straight line until it touches the edge of the larger shape. Keep the same color.
Change the  grid size as the input grid. The place the colored cell as shown in the input grid.

Use the same grid size as the test grid. There will be a larger object and a smaller object. Replicate the larger object with the same color and location as it has in the test grid. For the smaller object use the same color it has in the test grid but shift its position in one direction until it is next to the larger object.
Keep the grid the same size. Copy the shapes from the input grid. Move the smaller square to the right until it's touching the larger square.
smaller shape move along original axis tothe larger shape do that it touches. same colour, same size
Copy from input, move blue squares 3 squares to the right, submit.
use the grid size from the input then move the smaller box until its touching the bigger box (keep color's the same)

Resize to fit grid input size.  Recreate the larger object exactly as shown.  Move the smaller object either directly down so that it sits on top or connects to the larger object.  Use the same colors.
Resize the grid to match the sample.  Redraw the larger shape exactly.  Move the smaller shape so it touches the larger shape.  Keep it in line with the same row or column.  Keep the same colors.

Keep the same grid size as the input. There will be two shapes in the input - a smaller one and a larger one. In your answer, keep the larger one as it is and shift the smaller one in the direction of the larger one so that it is touching it. Keep the colours the same

I created a grid size Hight 15 width 15
I created the large shape from the task demo and brought the smaller shape next to the larger shape.

Create a grid exactly the same height and width as the original grid.  Copy the largest shape exactly as it is in the original, the size, colour and position must be the same.  Draw the smaller shape the same colour and size as the original but make it touch the edge of the larger shape.  If this means it moves vertically downwards, keep it in the same columns horizontally, if it has to move horizontally to tough the larger shape, keep it in the same vertical position as the original image.

Use the same size grid as the input.. In your answers, at every intersection where blocks overlap, the vertical color block must overlap the horizontal color block. Keep all colors the same except the overlapping block which must have the same color as the vertical blocks

Use the same size grid as the input and the same colours. There will be two objects. Leave the largest object in its original position. Move the smaller object as many squares directly to the left or right or up or down as it needs until it touches the bigger object, whilst remaining the same size.

You should see 2 colored shapes on a black background. Copy the input grid, then simply move the smaller shape straight over or down to touch the larger one. The larger one won't move. The place where the smaller shape originally was should now be black but the shape will be next to, and touching, the larger one.

Keeping the same grid size, leave the largest shape in the same place, and shift the smallest shape down or over so that it is in the same orientation, but making contact with the largest shape. Use the same colors.
Copy from the input grid. Then move the smaller object over to be adjacent to the larger object, either up, down, left, or right, depending on the objects' positioning.

Keep the grid the same as the demonstration image. Move/slide the smaller shape over so that it connects directly with the larger shape. If it is moving vertically then it needs to stay on the same vertical rows and the same goes for if the smaller shape is moving horizontal then it needs to stay in the same columns. Keep the colours the same.

Use the same size grid as the original. Determine which coloured object is the largest and retain it in the same size, colour and position in the new grid. Move the smaller object to the edge of the larger object along either the x or y axis, depending on whether the larger object is below it or to its side.
Use the same grid size as in the input. Observe the grids in the input and invert the object. Ensure the colours are the same for the invert.
copy from the grid and move the upper objects towards the lower object.

Match the Test input grid size. Move the individual colored boxes towards the solid object in the direction the individual boxes are extending. Keep the single boxes one space apart, with the closest being adjacent to the solid object.

The answer grid matches the size of the input grid. The input grid has a colored bar that will remain the same size and color in the answer. The additional squares must be shifted next to the bar in the direction they face but remain the same configuration.

Copy the grid and cells exactly.  Now, imagine the spaced cells of the same colour moving towards the solid line of cells.  Move the spaced cells along the "line of direction" they create until the first cell is adjacent to the solid line of cells.  Keep the original colour, spacing and orientaion of the spaced cells.

Copy the image (including colours and grid size)

Imagine that the dots are moving towards the line in the solution image. If the dots are diagonal, then the direction they are moving is also diagonal, following the first dot in the line.

Once they come into contact with the line they stop. The gap between each dot remains the same. Draw the result.
Imagine the 3 squares are an objecting moving in that direction. Create the pattern shown but with the first square of the "moving" object now directly in contact with the line of squares
Copy from input, move the orange squares all down by 5 squares, submit.
Move the dotted squares over so that the one on the right is touching the green line. Then space out the other two the same as in the original picture.
Find the long, solid line and move the dotted line until it connects with the solid line.

The input contains a wall, and an arrangement of three dots. Keep the color and position of the wall the same in your answer. Keeping the line of three dots on the same side of the wall as the input, shift it to touch the wall (keeping the same color).

Resize the grid to the input grid's. There will be an object that is a line and a series of objects that are the same color that make a dotted line. Slide the dotted line shapes in the same direction that they appear until they touch the long line object. Keep all the object colors the same.

resize the grid to be the same.  find the vertical or horizontal line and copy that to the new grid keeping the same dimensions and color. Now move the broken box pattern to but up against the long line.  if the pattern is a diagonal then you move it diagonal until it touches the long line.

The rule is to move the dots to be touching the other shape using the shortest path but maintaining the original spacing of the dots. So copy the grid then move the dots closer and fill in to match coloring. Next delete the original dots or fill them in with black.
Select correct number of squares. Draw two objects. Make sure that the objects are touching.
Use the same grid size as in the input.  There will be four objects in the input in your answer.  The input will remain the same.  Keep the colors the same.

Copy the grid.

You should see a long line of one color followed by individual blocks of one color with one empty space between them in a line.

If the blocks in a line with gaps were travelling towards the line in the fastest way (either straight or diagonally) match the blocks to where they would intersect. 

If the solid line is already touching the dotted line, then replicate the puzzle as it already is.
Copy the demonstration display exactly as it is.

grid size 14x13, draw a vertical line from the top left corner moving down 5 squares, in grey, place a yellow square 3 down 2 across, then place two more yellow squares in a diagonal line missing every other square.

Use the same grid size as the input. Identify the two shapes in the grid: one is composed of a continuous bar of singular colour and the other will appear as three singular squares uniformly spaced in a line (or a 'dotted' line). In your answer, shift the three singular squares as a line towards the solid bar until it makes contact. All colours and shapes remain the same. If the three squares are already in contact with the solid bar, nothing needs to be done, and simply replicate the image exactly as it is
Copy from input. Move left object all the way to the left of grid, and move right object all the way to the right of grid.

Keep the grid the same as the demonstration image. Move the boxed shapes to opposite sides of the grid so that the left hand box touches the left hand wall and the right hand box touches the right hand wall. Keep the colours the same.
Keep grid size the same. Keep the existing shapes the same size and colour, but move the one on the left completely over to the left side, and the one on the right completely over to the right side.

Repoduce the correct size grid mine was Hight12 x Width 16
